https://www.ibm.com/reports/data-breach-.markdown:
# IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024

The average cost of a data breach reached $4.88 million in 2024, a 10% increase over the prior year and the highest total ever recorded.

By industry sector:
- Energy and utilities: $5.29 million average breach cost (highest across sectors)
- Manufacturing: $4.73 million
- Healthcare: $9.77 million

By incident type:
- System intrusion: $4.62 million median
- Ransomware: $5.13 million median (excluding ransom payment)
- Accidental disclosure: $3.17 million median

Geographic breakdown for energy sector:
- North America: $6.21 million
- Europe: $4.84 million
- Asia-Pacific: $3.98 million

Raw quote: 'Energy sector breaches cost an average of $5.29 million in 2024, reflecting the high operational and regulatory costs associated with critical infrastructure incidents.'
https://www.ibm.com/reports/data-breach-.title: IBM Cost of a Data Breach Report 2024

https://www.verizon.com/business/resourc.markdown:
# Verizon 2024 Data Breach Investigations Report

The 2024 DBIR analyzed 30,458 security incidents and 10,626 confirmed data breaches across 94 countries.

Key findings for the energy sector:
- System intrusion pattern accounts for 72% of energy sector breaches
- Median financial impact of system intrusion: $46,000 (25th percentile) to $4.2M (75th percentile)
- Ransomware present in 32% of energy sector breaches
- OT-targeting incidents up 87% year-over-year

Raw quote: 'In the energy sector, system intrusion incidents have a median financial impact of $1.4M, with 25% of incidents exceeding $4.2M in total losses including downtime, remediation, and regulatory penalties.'
https://www.verizon.com/business/resourc.title: Verizon 2024 Data Breach Investigations Report
